what_it_does: A token-driven CSS stylesheet that implements the shared product chrome for phone-sized mockups and standalone screen artifacts, plus specialized layouts for unit views, decision cards, generic archetype renders, composed decision slides, explained presentations, a wayfinder index, and a graph instrument.
observations: Relies on external --dna-* CSS custom properties for spacing, type, radius, and color tokens.
Heavy use of color-mix(), backdrop-filter, radial gradients, and repeating-linear-gradient hatch patterns.
Includes @container and @media responsive composition rules, especially for .dv-view and .screen.ar.
Comments are extensive and read as design rationale, critique responses, and iteration notes (R1/R2/R3, STEP 1-5, worklist references).
Contains a generic archetype renderer (.ar-*) with tile, row, decided-moment, error, and ghost states.
Graph instrument (.gr-*) includes pan/zoom, animated dashed flows, selection pulse, and a rising plain-English card.
Wayfinder instrument (.wf-*) is a scrollable address index with chips, sections, and search input.
Many absolute-positioned chrome pieces: status bars, rails, hatches, action buttons, hotspots.
No CSS variables are defined in this file; it consumes tokens assumed to be provided elsewhere.
standouts: The .ar-* generic archetype renderer unifying decision-card styling across surfaces.
The .dv-* composed decision view with @container-based two-column reflow and a contained interface panel.
Inline adversarial-critique fix sections that document visual problems and their CSS remedies.
The graph instrument with animated .gr-flow traces, .gr-pan easing, and a bottom-sheet .gr-card.
The repeated gold-hatch and warm radial-ground motifs that create a consistent material language.
conventions: Class prefixes group by concern: .p-* product chrome, .ar-* archetype, .dv-* decision view, .ex-* explained, .wf-* wayfinder, .gr-* graph.
Token-driven sizing and color; no raw hex values, only var() and color-mix().
Mobile-first with breakpoints at 760px and 1180px, plus @container queries for frame-aware layout.
Glassmorphism using backdrop-filter and translucent backgrounds.
Stateful classes like .rec, .chosen, .open, .show, .write-error, .active drive visual differences.
Comments double as design decisions and iteration history.
concerns: All --dna-* tokens are external, so the file is not self-contained and will fail without the token layer.
Many highly specific overrides (e.g., .dv-hold .screen.ar, .screen.ar .ar-zone-visual) create specificity debt.
Comments reference external files and unresolved work (BACKLOG, flagged to projection, host contract) that are not visible here.
@container queries require an ancestor with container-type, which is not set in this file.
Some advanced features (color-mix, backdrop-filter) lack fallbacks for older browsers.
Iterative fix comments suggest significant churn and possible regressions in the visual system.
notes: A dense, design-rationale-heavy CSS manifest that encodes the entire phone/screen surface layer and several data-driven instruments in one shared stylesheet.
questions: Where are the --dna-* tokens defined that this file depends on?
What JavaScript or renderer applies classes like .screen.ar, .rec, .chosen, and .dv-compare-view?
How is the @container context established for .dv-view queries?
What is the exact content and structure of the referenced dna/application.json, modes.json, and substrate/wayfinder.json records?
What does the device-axis resolver mentioned in comments look like, and how will it replace the interim @media breakpoints?
What is the ConceptV line-icon set referenced for .uv-soc sockets?
What is the adversarial critique wf_ba99772c-083 and how is it tracked?
purpose_vs_actual: The header says it is the shared product chrome components linked by every app piece, driven by page CSS vars and records. The file does provide that shared chrome, but it also contains far more: full archetype-specific layouts, responsive presentation views, graph/wayfinder instruments, and iterative refinement patches. Purpose matches at the core but the scope is broader than the phone chrome alone.
apparent_themes: Shared component substrate over per-piece redrawing.
Material depth, layering, and signal economy.
Responsive/adaptive composition without JS class swapping.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to build a single CSS substrate that can render many application faces from data records and design tokens, while continuously refining visual craft through documented critique-and-fix cycles.
novelty: The combination of a generic archetype renderer, @container-based composed decision slides, inline adversarial-critique remediation notes, and a graph instrument with animated substrate geometry makes this more than a typical component stylesheet.
contribution: It contributes a comprehensive, token-driven visual layer for phone and screen surfaces, including reusable component classes, responsive layout rules, stateful decision-card affordances, and texture patterns that a fused system could keep as a shared styling substrate.
summary_for_embedding: A large token-driven CSS file defining phone and standalone screen product chrome, generic archetype decision-card layouts, composed presentation views, and wayfinder/graph instruments with extensive design-rationale comments and responsive rules.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a shared, live-themed CSS substrate for rendering multiple application screen archetypes from design tokens and data records.
